errorPage.body: We couldn't load this page. The record may be temporarily unavailable.

copyableEmail.caption: CC this on your next business email

home.subhead: A verifiable record of every business email you send — built passively, impossible to backdate.

mock.title: Your full public page.
mock.sub: Every badge click opens here. Real sender, real timestamps, real volume — shareable anywhere a counterparty might look.
mock.disclaimerRest: Each entry is verified at the moment of sending — not self-reported.
mock.lookupLabel: Check if your domain is on record
mock.lookupAria: Enter a domain to check if it's on record
mock.realOne: or see a real domain's page:

badge.title: Paste once. Lives in every email you send.
badge.sub: A small live image you drop into your email signature. It updates automatically as your history grows. One click opens your full public page.
badge.footnote: Works in Gmail, Outlook, Apple Mail, and anywhere else HTML signatures work.

privacy.title: Private by design.
privacy.body1: Your emails contain sensitive information. We never see it. The CC reaches us, we verify the sender, record the timestamp, and discard everything else.
privacy.body2: You choose which conversations count. Nothing is collected without an explicit CC — and you can access, export, or delete anything we hold, anytime.
privacy.subject: Email subject

cta.built: Built for freelancers, agencies, startups — anyone who needs to prove they've been here.

seal.trustLine: Every entry was verified at the moment of sending — not self-reported. This record only grows forward.
seal.sparklineAria: {count} verified emails in the last {days} days
seal.activitySummary: {count, plural, =0 {No verified emails in the last 30 days.} =1 {# verified email in the last 30 days.} other {# verified emails in the last 30 days.}}
seal.badgeIntro: A compact form of this page. Lives in email signatures and updates as the record grows — every click opens here.

unclaimed.noOutboundBody: <d>{name}</d> has been the recipient in {count} witnessed {count, plural, =1 {communication} other {communications}}. Other businesses are already emailing this domain and CCing Witnessed — but <d>{name}</d> hasn't started building its own outbound record yet.
unclaimed.noRecordBody: <d>{name}</d> hasn't been witnessed yet. CC <e>{addr}</e> on a business email and this page fills in — automatically, no account needed.
unclaimed.setupBody: Add Witnessed to every email your domain sends — once. One-time configuration in your mail provider, then this page builds itself in the background.
unclaimed.manualNote: Or try it now with a single CC:
unclaimed.startBody: Put this address in CC on your next business email. The record starts itself.

badge.howTo: Works in Gmail, Outlook, and Apple Mail. The badge stays clickable when pasted.

privacyPage.title: Privacy Policy
privacyPage.shortBody: We record only what's needed to verify that a business email was sent: the sender's domain, the recipient's domain, the timestamp, and a hash of the DKIM signature. We never see, store, or process the content of your emails. The CC is an explicit, voluntary act — nothing is collected without your deliberate action.
privacyPage.collectIntro: When you CC <addr>seal@witnessed.cc</addr> on an email, we record:
collectItems: the domain portion of your From address (e.g. acme.com)
the domain of the primary recipient
when the email was received by our system
a one-way hash used to verify authenticity; the original signature is discarded
privacyPage.neverIntro: The following are discarded immediately upon receipt and never stored:
neverItems: Personal names, addresses, or contact information
privacyPage.neverFoot: No human at Witnessed ever reads your emails. This is enforced architecturally — email content never reaches our database.
privacyPage.useIntro: The data we collect is used solely to:
useItems: Build and display the public page for your domain at witnessed.cc/b/yourdomain
Compute the verified business history metrics shown on that page
Power the verification record that proves your business has been operational over time
privacyPage.useFoot: We do not sell data, share it with third parties for advertising, or use it for any purpose other than the above.
privacyPage.consentBody: The CC is an explicit, voluntary act on each individual email. You are in full control of which emails contribute to your record. There is no passive collection — Witnessed only receives data when you deliberately include the address in a CC field.
privacyPage.publicBody: These pages are public by design. The domain name, event count, and first-seen date are visible to anyone who visits the page at witnessed.cc/b/yourdomain. This is the product — the public proof of business activity. If you do not want a public record for your domain, do not CC the address.
privacyPage.basisIntro: Under the EU General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR), we rely on the following lawful bases:
basisItems: building and maintaining a verified business history that allows senders to prove their domain has been operating over time, and allows recipients to evaluate that proof. We consider this interest proportionate because we store only domain-level metadata and offer a frictionless opt-out.
the CC is a deliberate, per-email action. Nothing is collected passively.
privacyPage.rightsIntro: If you control a domain that appears in our records — as sender or as recipient — you can exercise the following rights at any time, self-serve, via <link>witnessed.cc/rights</link>:
rightsItems: download everything we hold about your domain as a signed JSON export
permanently delete every record referencing your domain, as sender and as recipient
block future ingestion without deleting the existing record
the data is observational and rarely wrong; if you believe a specific record is inaccurate, contact us
the JSON export is structured and machine-readable
email us to pause processing while a dispute is resolved
privacyPage.rightsFoot: Ownership of the domain is proven via a DNS TXT record — the same mechanism used to verify sender authenticity. We answer rights requests within 30 days, as required by Art 12(3).
privacyPage.retentionBody: Your domain's history is retained for as long as the service operates, because the historical nature of the record is the product. You may purge all records at any time via <link>witnessed.cc/rights</link>. Erasure is permanent and irreversible.
privacyPage.subProcessorsIntro: We use a small set of vendors to operate the service. Each is bound by a Data Processing Agreement and processes data only on our instructions:
subProcessors: email routing (SMTP catch-all → Worker → our API)
privacyPage.subProcessorsFoot: We do not share data with any other third party. We do not use analytics, advertising, or fingerprinting services of any kind.
privacyPage.transfersBody: Where data is processed outside the European Economic Area, we rely on the European Commission's Standard Contractual Clauses and the EU-US Data Privacy Framework for our US-based sub-processors. You can request a copy of these safeguards by contacting us.
privacyPage.complaintBody: If you are in the EU/EEA and believe we have processed your data unlawfully, you have the right to lodge a complaint with your national supervisory authority. A list is maintained by the European Data Protection Board at edpb.europa.eu/about-edpb/about-edpb/members_en.
privacyPage.infraBody: Data is stored on Vercel Postgres (Neon), encrypted at rest and in transit. Access is restricted to the application layer. No employee has direct database access in production.
privacyPage.changesBody: If we make material changes, we will update the date at the top of this page. Continued use of the service after changes constitutes acceptance.

setupPage.metaDescription: Add Witnessed to every email your domain sends. One-time configuration for Google Workspace, Microsoft 365, and Outlook.
setupPage.title: Set it up once.
setupPage.intro: Witnessed only sees the emails you CC it on. This page turns that CC from a thing you remember into a thing that happens automatically — directly inside the mail provider your domain already uses.
setupPage.copyCaption: Copy this — paste it into the recipe below

workspace.bestFor: teams on Google Workspace with a custom domain. One rule covers every employee, no per-user installs.
steps: Sign in to admin.google.com with an admin account.
Find "Content compliance" and click "Configure" (or "Add another rule").
Email messages to affect: select Outbound only. Leave the expression list empty to match every outgoing message.
Under "If the above expressions match, do the following", choose "Modify message" → "Add more recipients" → "Add Bcc recipient" → enter seal@witnessed.cc.
Save. The rule activates within a few minutes.
workspace.outcome: Every email sent from your domain is automatically BCC'd to Witnessed. No employee action, no browser extensions, no per-message decisions.

m365.bestFor: teams on Exchange Online or Microsoft 365 with a custom domain. One transport rule covers the organization.
steps: Sign in to admin.exchange.microsoft.com with an admin account.
Mail flow → Rules → "+ Create a new rule".
Apply this rule if: "The sender is located" → Inside the organization. Add condition: "The recipient is located" → Outside the organization.
Do the following: "Bcc the message to" → enter seal@witnessed.cc.
Set rule mode to "Enforce". Activate and save.
m365.outcome: Every external email sent from your organization is automatically BCC'd to Witnessed. Internal mail is unaffected.

outlook.bestFor: individuals on Outlook classic (Windows or Mac) without admin access. The rule runs locally as you send.
steps: File → Manage Rules & Alerts → New Rule.
Under "Start from a blank rule" select "Apply rule on messages I send" → Next.
Leave all conditions unchecked to apply the rule to every message you send → Next → Yes (confirm).
Actions: tick "Cc the message to people or public group". Click the underlined link and enter seal@witnessed.cc → OK.
Skip exceptions → Next. Name the rule "Witnessed CC" → Finish.
outlook.outcome: Outlook adds the CC automatically on every outbound email you send. The rule only runs on the machine where it was created — repeat once on each device you use.

everyoneElse.eyebrow: Gmail personal, Apple Mail, Proton, others
everyoneElse.body: These providers don't yet support "always-BCC" rules natively. A one-click browser extension is on the way. In the meantime, the manual path works reliably — drop the address above in CC on any business email and it joins your record automatically.
everyoneElse.outcome: Paste seal@witnessed.cc into CC the first time; mail clients remember it for next time. You can add more as you send.

rightsPage.metaDescription: Access, opt out, or erase records for a domain you control. DNS-verified, self-serve.
rightsPage.title: Your rights
rightsPage.lede: Self-serve access, opt-out, and erasure for any domain you control. Ownership is proven with a short DNS TXT record — the same trust mechanism that underpins the whole service. No email, no ticket, no waiting.
rightsPage.step3Body: Add the record below to your domain's DNS. Once it resolves, click the button underneath to complete the action.
rightsPage.step3Note: DNS propagation usually takes less than a minute but can take up to a few hours depending on your provider. The challenge is valid for the rest of this UTC day and the next.

access.title: Access
access.sub: Download everything we hold about this domain (Art 15).

opt_out.title: Opt out
opt_out.sub: Block future records without deleting the past (Art 21).

erasure.title: Erase
erasure.sub: Permanently delete every record referencing this domain (Art 17).

errors.invalid_domain: That doesn't look like a valid domain. Use the bare form, e.g. acme.com.
errors.no_txt_record: No TXT record found at the expected host. Check your DNS and wait a minute for propagation.
errors.dns_error: We couldn't reach your DNS. Try again in a moment.
errors.challenge_mismatch: A TXT record exists but the value doesn't match. Make sure you copied the full value and that no characters were altered by your DNS panel.
errors.network_error: Couldn't reach Witnessed. Check your connection and try again.
errors.server_error: Something went wrong on our side. Try again in a moment.

success.accessBody: Here is everything we hold about this domain. Download the JSON file or inspect it below.
success.optOutBody: This domain has been added to our denylist. Future inbound emails involving it as sender or primary recipient will be silently dropped. Existing records remain — use Erase if you also want them purged.
success.erasureBody: We deleted {sender} events where this domain was the sender and {receiver} events where it was a recipient. Other senders' event counts were adjusted accordingly. The domain is now on the denylist so it cannot be re-ingested.

rightsPage.whyTxtBody: DNS is the existing proof of who controls a domain. If you can publish a record under it, you have administrative control — exactly what we need to trust a rights request. Witnessed never asks for an account, so this is the lightest-weight way to bind a request to an actual owner.
rightsPage.timingTitle: How fast do these take effect?
rightsPage.timingBody: Opt-out and erasure apply immediately on verification. Our inbound pipeline re-checks the denylist on every email, so even a CC arriving seconds after erasure will be dropped. Access requests return the live state at the moment of verification.
rightsPage.scopeTitle: What a rights request does and doesn't cover
rightsPage.scopeBody: A verified request covers only the domain named in it. If you control several domains you need to verify each one separately. Erasure removes references to the verified domain even on other senders' records; it does not delete those other senders' accounts or reveal anything about them.
rightsPage.contactIntro: If anything about this flow isn't clear, or you believe we're processing data about you or your domain that you can't see here, reach out:

termsPage.title: Terms of Service
termsPage.whatBody: Witnessed (witnessed.cc) is a service that builds a verified business history for email domains. When you CC seal@witnessed.cc on a business email, we verify the DKIM signature, record the metadata, and add it to the public proof-of-business record for your domain.
termsPage.acceptanceBody: By using this service — including by CCing our address, visiting a page at witnessed.cc/b/yourdomain, or using the verification API — you agree to these terms. If you do not agree, do not use the service.
termsPage.useIntro: You agree not to use Witnessed to:
useItems: Generate false or misleading business history (e.g. coordinated networks of domains CCing each other to manufacture artificial credibility)
Impersonate another business or domain you do not control
Attempt to manipulate, reverse-engineer, or tamper with the verification system
Abuse the service in a way that degrades performance for other users
termsPage.useFoot: We reserve the right to remove, flag, or permanently downgrade records associated with domains found to be engaging in manipulation.
termsPage.publicBody: Once your domain has been witnessed, a public page exists at witnessed.cc/b/yourdomain. This page is publicly accessible and indexed. You agree that building a record using our service means this page may be visible to anyone.
termsPage.provesTitle: What Witnessed proves and does not prove
termsPage.provesBody1: Witnessed proves that a domain has been sending DKIM-authenticated email to real counterparties over a period of time. It does not prove legal standing, financial solvency, compliance with any regulation, or the quality or legitimacy of the underlying business activity.
termsPage.provesBody2: Verified status is a signal, not a guarantee. Do not rely on a Witnessed record as the sole basis for a high-stakes business decision.
termsPage.availabilityBody: We provide Witnessed on an as-is, as-available basis. We do not guarantee uptime, data retention beyond reasonable commercial practice, or continuity of the service. We will make reasonable efforts to preserve records, but cannot be held liable for data loss.
termsPage.liabilityBody: To the maximum extent permitted by law, Witnessed and its operators are not liable for any indirect, incidental, special, consequential, or punitive damages arising from your use of the service, including but not limited to loss of business, loss of data, or reliance on a verified record.
termsPage.terminationBody1: We may suspend or terminate access to the service at any time, with or without notice, for conduct that violates these terms or that we determine is harmful to the integrity of the record.
termsPage.terminationBody2: You may request deletion of your domain's record at any time. See our <link>Privacy Policy</link> for details.
termsPage.changesBody: We may update these terms from time to time. We will update the date at the top of this page. Continued use of the service after changes constitutes acceptance of the new terms.
